steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1: contour and box modeling process thousands of shapes. Sentence 2: these methods are more economical than surface models, which need millions of polygons. Fewer shapes processed = lower computing cost: the second sentence is a consequence of the first.

steps.title: Pick the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect/consequence: the lower shape count (cause) makes these methods more economical (effect).
  • "Instead,": signals substitution or contradiction. The second sentence is not opposed to the first; it flows from it. ✗
  • "Specifically,": signals a narrowing detail or example of the prior claim. The economy comparison is a result, not a specification of thousands of shapes. ✗
  • "As such,": signals consequence — "because of this." The economy is a direct result of processing fewer shapes. ✓
  • "For example,": signals a specific instance illustrating a general claim. The economy comparison is not an example of processing thousands of shapes. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
